_depthConvention: Depth is a negative integer in [-60..-1]. Ranges are written [shallowest, deepest] = [closerToZero, moreNegative]. A depth d is in [a,b] iff b <= d <= a.

HumanAdapted.reason: human retrofits exist only down to the city zone (-11)

HumanFrontier.reason: abandoned human outposts below the city, do not penetrate the lower zones

Goblins.reason: goblin tribes scavenge the upper-mid zone

Ratfolk.reason: ratfolk thrive in the Cooling zone, absent in deepest layers

HumanGarrison.reason: city guard patrols only the topmost levels (env.md §2.4)

HumanExiles.reason: exiles dwell below the point of no return, but cannot survive the deepest reheated zone in numbers

DwarvenRemains.reason: remains of evacuated dwarves layer the mid and lower depths

DeepHostile.reason: deep-hostile presence begins after the city zone (-10/-11)

Natural.reason: installed light fixtures contradict untouched Natural origin; prefer Campfire / Geothermal / CrystalGlow / NearDark

_notes: softMinDepth means: material is only plausible at or below the given depth (more negative). Combination above it generates a warning, not a hard reject.
Range mismatches outside originDepthRange/occupantDepthRange are hard rejects — they break lore.
Thermal-zone material restrictions are hard rejects unless listed in softMinDepth.
